title: Festivals & events
subtitle: 40 celebrations through the year — pick a day to see what's on.

festivals.name: Lunar New Year
festivals.description: Korea's most important traditional holiday, celebrated with family reunions, special foods, and ancestral rites. The entire nation observes this three-day public holiday.

festivals.name: Jinhae Cherry Blossom Festival
festivals.description: Korea's most famous cherry blossom celebration featuring over 350,000 cherry trees blooming around a scenic navy port. The festival transforms the city with illuminated trees and cultural events.

festivals.name: Boryeong Mud Festival
festivals.description: A unique beach festival where visitors cover themselves in mineral-rich mud from local clay, followed by games, concerts, and swimming. It's one of Asia's most eccentric and fun summer events.

festivals.name: Buddha's Birthday
festivals.description: A national holiday celebrating Buddha's birth with lantern festivals at temples nationwide and colorful street processions. Major temples hold special ceremonies and the streets are decorated with elaborate lanterns.

festivals.name: Jeju Fire Festival
festivals.description: A spring festival celebrating the burning of dry grass on Jeju's volcanic landscape, blending ancient tradition with modern performances. The festival includes folk games, fireworks, and cultural events.

festivals.name: Busan International Film Festival
festivals.description: Asia's largest film festival attracting international cineastes and celebrities to Korea's busiest port city. It showcases over 300 films from around the world and hosts major awards ceremonies.

festivals.name: Gwangju Kimchi Festival
festivals.description: Celebrating Korea's iconic fermented vegetable dish with cooking competitions, tastings, and cultural performances. Visitors can make their own kimchi and learn traditional preservation techniques.

festivals.name: Chuseok
festivals.description: Korea's second most important holiday, a harvest festival when families return home for ancestral rites and feasts. The entire nation observes this three-day public holiday with minimal business activity.

festivals.name: Seoul Lantern Festival
festivals.description: The Cheonggyecheon stream is transformed with thousands of artistic lanterns creating a magical atmosphere through the heart of the capital. The festival celebrates traditional and contemporary lantern art.

festivals.name: Andong Mask Dance Festival
festivals.description: One of Korea's most important traditional cultural festivals featuring ancient mask dances and rituals passed down for centuries. International performers join Korean artists in celebrating this UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage.

festivals.name: Daegu Chimac Festival
festivals.description: A celebration of Korea's beloved fried chicken and beer pairing with cooking contests, performances, and street food. The festival attracts hundreds of thousands of food lovers to taste premium chimac.

festivals.name: Icheon Ceramic Festival
festivals.description: Celebrating Korea's ceramics capital with exhibitions, workshops, and competitions showcasing traditional and contemporary pottery. Visitors can try their hand at the potter's wheel and purchase unique ceramic pieces.

festivals.name: Muju Firefly Festival
festivals.description: A summer festival celebrating the magical return of fireflies to the Muju valley with nighttime viewing tours and traditional cultural performances. Visitors experience the pristine ecology of this rural mountain region.

festivals.name: Incheon International Jazz Festival
festivals.description: One of Asia's premier jazz festivals bringing world-class musicians to the port city for a week of concerts and performances. The festival celebrates both traditional and contemporary jazz across multiple venues.

festivals.name: Namwon Chunhyang Festival
festivals.description: Celebrating the legendary love story of Chunhyang with traditional performances, costume parades, and reenactments of classical Korean theater. The festival keeps alive an important part of Korean literary heritage.

festivals.name: Gangreung Coffee Festival
festivals.description: Korea's premier coffee celebration in the mountainous region famous for specialty coffee cultivation. The festival features barista competitions, tastings, and cultural performances.

festivals.name: Jeonju International Film Festival
festivals.description: A prestigious film festival in Korea's cultural capital showcasing innovative independent and art cinema from around the world. The festival celebrates cinematic artistry and discovery.

festivals.name: Suncheon Bay Ecological Festival
festivals.description: Celebrating the wetlands ecosystem with nature walks, cultural performances, and environmental education in one of Asia's most beautiful bays. The festival promotes ecological conservation and sustainable tourism.

festivals.name: Korean Foundation Day
festivals.description: A national holiday commemorating the legendary founding of Gojoseon in 2333 BCE, Korea's first kingdom. The day features cultural celebrations and flag displays throughout the country.

festivals.name: Jindo Haenyeo Diving Festival
festivals.description: Celebrating the legendary female divers who harvest seafood by breath-holding, a UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage. Visitors can watch spectacular diving demonstrations and witness this ancient tradition.

festivals.name: Ulsan Whale Festival
festivals.description: Celebrating the return of migratory whales to Korean waters with performances, exhibitions, and marine education. The festival honors Korea's maritime heritage and conservation efforts.

festivals.name: Daejeon Science Festival
festivals.description: Korea's hub of science and technology celebrates innovation with interactive exhibits, demonstrations, and performances. The festival inspires public interest in STEM fields through engaging entertainment.

festivals.name: Gimhae Gaya Cultural Festival
festivals.description: Celebrating the ancient Gaya Kingdom with historical reenactments, traditional performances, and archaeological exhibitions. The festival preserves the legacy of this influential coastal civilization.

festivals.name: Hwacheon Sancheoneo Ice Festival
festivals.description: Korea's most famous winter festival on a frozen river where visitors can ice fish for trout and enjoy winter games. The festival attracts hundreds of thousands with its unique ice activities and traditional performances.

festivals.name: Chungju International Film Festival
festivals.description: A respected film festival showcasing quality cinema and emerging filmmakers in Korea's central region. The festival celebrates cinematic excellence and cultural exchange.

festivals.name: Memorial Day
festivals.description: A national holiday honoring those who died in wars and military service. Citizens observe moments of silence and visit memorials throughout the country.

festivals.name: Boshinggak New Year Bell Ringing
festivals.description: Ringing the historic Bosingak bell at midnight to welcome the new year is a Seoul tradition with thousands gathering for performances and celebrations. The ceremony symbolizes hope and renewal.

festivals.name: Boseong Green Tea Festival
festivals.description: Celebrating Korea's premier tea-growing region with picking demonstrations, tastings, and cultural performances among scenic emerald tea fields. Visitors experience traditional tea culture and can harvest their own leaves.

festivals.name: Wonju Paper Arts Festival
festivals.description: Celebrating traditional Korean paper (hanji) craftsmanship with exhibitions, workshops, and performances by master artisans. Visitors can create their own paper art and purchase handmade items.

festivals.name: Gwangju Biennale
festivals.description: One of Asia's most important contemporary art exhibitions showcasing innovative works and addressing global themes. The biennale elevates Gwangju as a center for contemporary Asian art.

festivals.name: Liberation Day
festivals.description: A national holiday commemorating Korea's liberation from Japanese colonial rule in 1945. The day features flag displays, patriotic ceremonies, and cultural celebrations.

festivals.name: Pohang Light Festival
festivals.description: Transforming a coastal industrial city with spectacular light installations and performances creating a magical winter atmosphere. The festival celebrates creativity and innovation through light art.

festivals.name: Jeonju Hanok Festival
festivals.description: Celebrating Korea's largest traditional hanok village with street performances, costume experiences, and cultural activities. Visitors immerse themselves in historical atmosphere and traditional arts.

festivals.name: Damyang Bamboo Festival
festivals.description: Celebrating bamboo culture in Korea's premier bamboo forest with performances, crafts, and food made from bamboo. Visitors can walk through the scenic forest and learn about traditional bamboo uses.

festivals.name: Seoul Jazz Festival
festivals.description: Korea's capital celebrates world-class jazz with performances by international and Korean artists across multiple venues. The festival brings sophisticated music and cultural exchange to central Seoul.

festivals.name: Giheung Strawberry Festival
festivals.description: Celebrating Korea's strawberry season with tastings, picking experiences, and cultural performances. The festival showcases the region's agricultural heritage and winter harvest.

festivals.name: Children's Day
festivals.description: A national holiday celebrating children with special events, performances, and festivities throughout the country. Parks and venues offer free or discounted admission for families.

festivals.name: Myeongdong Street Festival
festivals.description: Transforming Seoul's busy shopping district with street performances, flash mobs, and cultural celebrations. The festival brings artistic energy to one of Korea's most vibrant commercial areas.

festivals.name: Incheon Pentaport Rock Festival
festivals.description: Korea's premier rock and indie music festival attracting international and domestic bands to the port city. The festival showcases diverse musical styles and alternative culture.

festivals.name: Hangeul Day
festivals.description: A national holiday celebrating the invention of Hangeul, Korea's alphabet, in 1443. The day features cultural events highlighting Korean language and literary heritage.
